questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: What must not be done by Ram?

choices.a: Only produce 'quickie-quick' as it gives highest profits
choices.b: Increase the production for 'quickie-quick' and ask the supplier for better quality raw material
choices.c: Maintain the level of production for 'quickie-quick' and ask the supplier for better quality raw material
choices.d: Reduce the production of 'quickie-quick1 till committee submits its report
choices.e: Stop production of 'quickie-quick' till committee, submits its report

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Rahim set-up a 'brand' committee comprising a few selected' managers, headed by Robert. The committee proposed that IBM should continue to manufacture the three differently branded products. It also proposed to recruit a new brand manager for improving brand image of the products. It agreed with Rahim that the company should increase the price. Rocket Singh, head of sales, was confused because he realised that customers were miffed with 'run-of-the-mill' and 'maxi-max'. What should Rocket Singh do?

choices.a: Launch one more product to increase sales
choices.c: Launch a campaign on social media to increase awareness about company's products
choices.d: Send an anonymous e-mail to all the employees highlighting customer dissatisfaction
choices.e: Conduct a research study to find out the reason for customers dissatisfaction

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Some the-managers in production department were discussing the problems faced in shipping products in time. They complained that they had to undertake responsibility of creating financial and marketing plans in addition to responsibility of production planning. At the same time, finance and marketing managers were to be involved in preparing production plans. It was expected that this will reduce customer complaints. It was rumoured that these changes were initiated by the managers educated in the US. Which of the following is the best possible course of action available to the affected manager?

choices.a: Create a union to safeguard their rights
choices.b: File a low suit against the company
choices.c: Create a forum for discussion and resolution of issues
choices.d: Register a complaint with the human rights body
choices.e: Go for further education to the US

solutions.solution: It is inferred from the passage that the bidding has been done according to government policies and in the benefit of the citizens. According to the minister, the loss that the media is talking about is national.
According to the minister's view, it is correct to say that media should not act as a reporter, prosecutor and judge at the same time, as it may affect the goodwill and policies of the Government.
Corporate managers, the minister and the head of the nation all are responsible for the issue, as the one who disburses the responsibility is held equally responsible to the one who is fulfilling it.
Option (c) seems to be the only possible reason because both the instances happened over the same period of time.

Option (b) seems to be the correct answer in the last lines of the statement. It is mentioned 'Despite the success of the treatment, most doctors have not embraced the surgery.' This means that there is definitely some risk of life that's why doctors might not easily suggest Gastric bypass surgery and obesity is one of the leading contributors to heart disease and hypertension because from the starting line we can understand that it usually happens with obese people.
Both Statements I and II are required to calculate the profit/loss.

Contribution of total online store accounts for 50%. ∴ Total online contribution = 21000/2 = 10500. Now, Additional cost for Red/unit = 5500/275 = 20. Additional cost for Yellow/unit = 3100/100 = 31. Additional cost for Green/unit = 4800/80 = 60. Additional cost for Violet/unit = 2400/80 = 30. ∴ Total additional cost for online = (200 × 20 + 50 × 31 + 50 × 60 + 50 × 30) = 4000 + 1550 + 3000 + 1500 = 10050. ∴ Profit = Contribution – Total additional cost = 10500 – 10050 = 450
